full_prompt: Design a single 3D-printable centrifugal pump impeller and nozzle manifold for a fountain delivery challenge. The pump must deliver a minimum flow rate of 30 L/min at a total dynamic head of 3.0 m of water (29.4 kPa) when driven by a 12 VDC motor of 120 W maximum shaft input, using only approved polymers. The nozzle manifold must include four independently valved outlets to feed a height-shooter nozzle (12 mm exit), a wide-fan spray nozzle (5 mm slit), a target-jet nozzle (6 mm exit), and an accuracy-task pop-up nozzle (8 mm exit). All components must fit within a 120-minute assembly window and use only printed PLA or PETG parts plus standard O-ring seals. The impeller is a semi-open centrifugal type with outer diameter 60 mm, inlet eye 20 mm, blade count 6, blade outlet angle 25 degrees, and blade thickness 2.0 mm, mounted on a 5 mm keyed shaft. The system must simultaneously satisfy: (R1) delivered flow at 3.0 m head must be at least 30 L/min at the pump BEP, evaluated via head-flow curve from CFD or affinity laws, (R2) required shaft power at the operating point must not exceed 60 W to leave motor margin, (R3) maximum hoop stress in the impeller at 3000 rpm rotational speed must not exceed 25 MPa which is PLA yield divided by a 2.0 safety factor, (R4) maximum deflection at the impeller tip under centrifugal load must not exceed 0.3 mm to preserve clearance, and (R5) no impeller mode shape may have a natural frequency within +/- 20% of the operating speed (50 Hz) to avoid resonance. Fluid analysis (CFD or 1D affinity) is required to compute head-flow curve; structural FEA is required for R3 and R4; vibration (modal) is required for R5. Thermal and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 of the impeller and manifold as separate bodies plus a performance report with head-flow curve, BEP, shaft power, impeller stress, deflection, and first three natural frequencies.
short_prompt:
Design a 3D-printable semi-open centrifugal pump impeller plus a 4-outlet nozzle manifold for a fountain-delivery challenge, built from printed PLA/PETG and driven by a 12 VDC, 120 W max motor. The impeller is on the order of 60 mm OD with a 20 mm inlet eye on a 5 mm keyed shaft; the manifold has four independently valved outlets feeding a height-shooter, a fan spray, a target jet, and a pop-up nozzle.

Load cases.
- LC1 design operating point: 30 L/min at 3.0 m H2O head (29.4 kPa) at 3000 rpm.
- LC2 centrifugal structural: body force on the impeller at 3000 rpm.
- LC3 modal: operating forcing at 50 Hz, require +/-20% separation.

Shaft power at the operating point is the binding motor-budget constraint.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: BEP head-flow curve can be generated by affinity-law scaling from a baseline impeller performance test, without full CFD.

prompt.title: PLA centrifugal pump + nozzle manifold
prompt.brief: Design a PLA 3D-printed centrifugal impeller (60 mm OD, 6 blades) and 4-outlet nozzle manifold delivering 30 L/min at 3.0 m head on 120 W motor, passing PLA stress, deflection, and resonance separation.

boundary_conditions.shaft: torque reaction at 5 mm keyed shaft
boundary_conditions.casing: annular rigid casing wall around impeller

objective.primary: meet flow-head target at <=60 W shaft power

pass_fail_criteria.rule: each natural frequency f_i must satisfy |f_i - 50 Hz| / 50 Hz >= 0.20, i.e. f_i outside 40-60 Hz band, for i=1..3

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: second-order tet for impeller, shell for manifold

eval_coverage: coupled CFD pump + structural FEA + modal
